message:
chore(beads): file ft-dvgzi.1 + .2; progress comment on ft-dvgzi

3598a9618 lands the WeztermSubprocessFixture (foundation for full
snapshot_e2e migration AND ft-2funa watchdog fault injection). The
2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs rewrite is structurally non-trivial — per-
test rework needed for pane-id discovery, deterministic PTY content,
mux-restart restore semantics, and renderer steady-state. Decomposed:
- ft-dvgzi.1 (P2): author 3-5 NEW fixture-driven snapshot tests
- ft-dvgzi.2 (P3): piecemeal migrate the existing 2058 lines, gated
  on .1 surfacing LayoutRestorer API gaps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-4o5mb.6","title":"[conformance] wa.events_annotate / wa.events_triage / wa.events_label lack schema+golden roundtrip","description":"Manifest coverage audit shows the event mutator trio still lacks per-tool MCP conformance goldens. Existing conformance files cover wa.events, wa.reserve/reservations/release, wa.state, wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for, wa.mission_*, and wa.tx_*; no integration harness currently freezes wa.events_annotate, wa.events_triage, or wa.events_label success envelopes plus framework invalid-params behavior against tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json. Fix: add one focused integration test file that seeds a real event row, freezes each tool's live input schema, captures canonical success envelopes, and records one representative boundary-invalid failure per tool into golden_robot_envelope/wa_event_mutations_conformance.json.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:43:21.762917Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:11:30.384053Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:11:30.383758Z","close_reason":"Partial verify in 3bad3568: added ignored wa.event mutator conformance skeleton pending reproducible golden lane","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","mcp","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:43:21.762917Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3630,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocked on verification in this turn: new wa.events_annotate / wa.events_triage / wa.events_label conformance harness is added at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_event_mutations_mcp_conformance.rs and formats cleanly, but UPDATE_GOLDEN local bypass spent >10 minutes compiling frankenterm-core without reaching test execution or writing tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_event_mutations_conformance.json. Remote no-run also failed on the known openssl-sys worker mismatch: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang does not exist on Linux workers.","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4r2ib","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.405716Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.545322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:16:34.544924Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0761bb33: mission dispatch now calls WorkflowRunner::handle_detection and returns real start metadata","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-4rsog","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347: SPMC send_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel on slow-consumer backpressure","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpmcProducer::send_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits shared.not_full[full_idx].notified() at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347. The docs promise pre-cancel and cancel during blocked notified.await return Err(value), but a cancellation after the future has suspended does not wake the Notify. If one consumer queue stays full and no consumer drains after cancellation, the producer can hang indefinitely despite the caller Cx being cancelled.

Expected fix direction: use the same select-race/cx-aware notify helper as the SPSC sender; add a regression where one SPMC consumer is full, send_with_cx blocks, cx is cancelled, and no consumer drain occurs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:23.486857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:16:03.029724Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-4ua6b","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262: SPSC recv_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_empty","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpscConsumer::recv_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits a plain asupersync Notify at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262. The docs state that a cancel during the blocked wait breaks out and returns None, but a mid-flight cancellation does not wake this notify wait. If the queue is empty and no producer sends or closes after cancellation, the receiver stays suspended. This is especially risky because runtime persistence currently waits on the non-cx recv path while shutdown_flag only gets checked after a message arrives.

Expected fix direction: race not_empty.notified() against a cx-cancel watcher or route through a common cx-aware notify wait; add a regression with empty queue, blocked recv_with_cx, then cancel without send/close.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:16.399653Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:15:32.995909Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-4rsog","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347: SPMC send_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel on slow-consumer backpressure","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpmcProducer::send_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits shared.not_full[full_idx].notified() at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:347. The docs promise pre-cancel and cancel during blocked notified.await return Err(value), but a cancellation after the future has suspended does not wake the Notify. If one consumer queue stays full and no consumer drains after cancellation, the producer can hang indefinitely despite the caller Cx being cancelled.

Expected fix direction: use the same select-race/cx-aware notify helper as the SPSC sender; add a regression where one SPMC consumer is full, send_with_cx blocks, cx is cancelled, and no consumer drain occurs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:23.486857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:43.374739Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:43.374522Z","close_reason":"Fixed spmc send_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-4ua6b","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262: SPSC recv_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_empty","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpscConsumer::recv_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits a plain asupersync Notify at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:262. The docs state that a cancel during the blocked wait breaks out and returns None, but a mid-flight cancellation does not wake this notify wait. If the queue is empty and no producer sends or closes after cancellation, the receiver stays suspended. This is especially risky because runtime persistence currently waits on the non-cx recv path while shutdown_flag only gets checked after a message arrives.

Expected fix direction: race not_empty.notified() against a cx-cancel watcher or route through a common cx-aware notify wait; add a regression with empty queue, blocked recv_with_cx, then cancel without send/close.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:16.399653Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:40.241522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:40.241292Z","close_reason":"Fixed spsc recv_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843379Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 05c8d36b (Option 2 from the bead). Semantic invariant polls>=1 remains enforced in assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477 (runs against the live envelope at :733, BEFORE canonicalization). canonicalize() at :480 now zeroes polls alongside now/elapsed_ms/captured_at so the byte-stable golden only freezes structural presence, not scheduler-timing-dependent count. wa_wait_for.json updated to polls:0 to match. Only golden in the tree carrying a polls key is wa_wait_for.json, so the canonicalize rule is unambiguous and cannot mask a real regression in another tool's envelope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
@@ -128,7 +128,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-8ybme","title":"[scan_pipeline] process_chunk allows append-after-flush-pending growth","description":"ChunkedPipelineState::process_chunk only exposes should_flush() as an advisory signal. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:221-223 and 450-516, callers can keep appending after the active raw-data buffer has already crossed max_buffer_bytes, so a stalled or forgotten flusher grows trigger_data_buffer/uncompressed_buffer without bound.

Repro:
1. let pipeline = ScanPipeline::default();
2. let mut state = ChunkedPipelineState::new(64);
3. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'x'; 80], &mut state);
4. state.should_flush() == true
5. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'y'; 80], &mut state);
6. raw replay buffer keeps growing instead of failing fast or forcing a flush.

Expected: once flush is pending, a subsequent non-empty process_chunk call should refuse to accumulate more bytes rather than silently converting backpressure into unbounded memory growth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:00.532379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.219221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.218185Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 658b945a: scan_pipeline now fails fast when flush is pending","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-94juo","title":"[modes-of-reasoning] no CI guard against new sub-crate cycles — cargo cycles caught 3x during ft-y0loj extractions; needs pre-flight check","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.506909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:26:27.804489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:26:27.804218Z","close_reason":"Shipped at HEAD 72f08f98. scripts/check_workspace_cycles.sh + check_workspace_cycles.py implement a DFS cycle detector over cargo metadata --format-version=1 --no-deps. Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml as new step 'Workspace dep-graph cycle guard (ft-94juo)' immediately after ft-zoxxq.4 mux-interface guard. Verified: HEAD smoke-test detected 64 workspace members + 215 intra-workspace prod/build edges with ZERO cycles (exit 0). Synthetic 2-package mutual-dep JSON correctly detected with full edge trail (exit 1). Skips dev-dep edges to match cargo's own forgiveness. Surfaces FULL cycle with kind annotation rather than cargo's single-edge-pair error, plus a hint pointing at ft-usvnt resource-types extraction as the canonical resolution pattern. Mirror of ft-zoxxq.4 guard pattern (8d9fc273).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-9aa76","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:492: SPMC recv_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_empty","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpmcConsumer::recv_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits shared.not_empty[consumer_idx].notified() at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:492. The docs say mid-wait cancel returns None, but there is no select-race around the Notify wait. If a consumer is waiting on an empty queue and the caller cancels without any later send/close notification, the task remains suspended.

Expected fix direction: race the Notify wait against cx cancellation, preferably via a common helper shared with SPSC recv/send; add a LabRuntime test with an empty SPMC queue and cancellation after the receiver is parked.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:30.298983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:15:02.967661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Real hazard: SpmcConsumer::recv_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits shared.not_empty[consumer_idx].notified() at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:492. The docs say mid-wait cancel returns None, but there is no select-race around the Notify wait. If a consumer is waiting on an empty queue and the caller cancels without any later send/close notification, the task remains suspended.

Expected fix direction: race the Notify wait against cx cancellation, preferably via a common helper shared with SPSC recv/send; add a LabRuntime test with an empty SPMC queue and cancellation after the receiver is parked.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:30.298983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:46.627150Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:46.626856Z","close_reason":"Fixed spmc recv_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9ak8l","title":"frankenterm-core-error-types tests miss serde_json dependency","description":"cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core-error-types --all-targets -- -D warnings fails because error_codes.rs tests call serde_json but the extracted leaf crate does not declare serde_json. Add the workspace serde_json dependency and verify clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:06:40.590478Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:06:45.004064Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:06:45.003733Z","close_reason":"Added serde_json workspace dependency and verified frankenterm-core-error-types clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9d4a3","title":"[MEDIUM] EventBus stats overreport queued backlog after subscribers drain channel","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:655-674 and 1942-1970. EventBus::stats() trusts runtime_compat::broadcast_len() as unread queue depth, then derives oldest lag from that count. Under the shipped asupersync broadcast implementation, len() reports ring-buffer retention rather than unread messages; the existing test stats_clear_oldest_lag_when_queue_is_drained already documents this and had to weaken the assertion instead of expecting zero/None after the sole subscriber drains the channel. Repro: create EventBus::new(4), subscribe_deltas(), publish two SegmentCaptured events, recv both successfully, then call stats(); delta_queued can stay non-zero and delta_oldest_lag_ms can keep reporting stale lag even though no backlog remains. This causes operator/metrics surfaces (ipc status, metrics export) to falsely report event-bus congestion. Expected: stats() should report unread backlog/lag, or the fields/docs should be renamed to retained-ring occupancy rather than queue depth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:14:13.498770Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232646Z","close_reason":"Track unread backlog from slowest subscriber instead of retained ring occupancy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["patterns","review"]}

 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
-{"id":"ft-dvgzi","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs: 2058-line "e2e" suite uses MockWezterm — should drive a real WeztermClient subprocess for snapshot/restore","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs (2058 lines) is named e2e but
substitutes MockWezterm for the real pane mux at every test entry point:
  20: use frankenterm_core::wezterm::{MockWezterm, ...};
  752, 1073, 1302, 1599, 1773, 2017: let wezterm = Arc::new(MockWezterm::new());

## Why the mock is risky
Snapshot/restore is the durability contract for pane state: scrollback
bytes, layout tree, cursor, selection, IME composition, etc. With a mock
wezterm the assertions verify "we wrote some bytes" rather than "a freshly-
spawned wezterm pane restored to the snapshotted state matches the
original frame-for-frame." The 2026-04-26 e2e_replay-related failures and
the ft-1memj.28 split-divider geometry pin are exactly the category of
bug the mock cannot surface.

Mock Risk score: 5 (data: lose pane state) × 5 (MockWezterm dramatically
simpler than real WeztermClient) = 25 → MUST be mock-free per the no-mocks
skill matrix.

## Real fixture available
crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:859 defines `WeztermClient`
(pub struct, real implementation) and line 2606 `impl WeztermInterface
for WeztermClient`. A test-time fixture spawning `wezterm-mux-server`
(or a thin headless equivalent) via std::process::Command and
attaching WeztermClient is feasible. tests/common/lab.rs already
hosts the harness scaffolding.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `WeztermSubprocessFixture` to tests/common/lab.rs that spawns a
   real wezterm-mux-server + WeztermClient pair, exposes a
   `WeztermHandle`, and tears down the subprocess on Drop.
2. Migrate ONE snapshot_e2e test at a time to the real fixture.
3. Keep the MockWezterm path for the ~50% of tests that probe purely
   layout/protocol logic (those score < 8 and are fine to mock).
4. Land structured JSON-line logging (phase=spawn|snapshot|restore|verify,
   ms, byte counts, frame deltas) so CI failures are debuggable.
5. Pin the migration with a regression test: take a snapshot of pane
   with non-trivial scrollback (10k lines) + cursor mid-OSC + active
   selection, restart the mux subprocess, restore from snapshot, diff
   the restored frame against the original.

## Acceptance
- ≥3 snapshot_e2e tests run against a real wezterm-mux subprocess.
- Diff between original and restored frame ≤ comparator tolerance.
- No regression: existing MockWezterm tests still green.

## Out of scope
- Migrating the entire 2058-line file (incremental).
- Modifying WeztermClient or MockWezterm themselves.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:17.294418Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:20.646158Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","session-persistence","snapshot","test"]}

## Suggested approach
1. Add `WeztermSubprocessFixture` to tests/common/lab.rs that spawns a

   `WeztermHandle`, and tears down the subprocess on Drop.
2. Migrate ONE snapshot_e2e test at a time to the real fixture.
3. Keep the MockWezterm path for the ~50% of tests that probe purely
   layout/protocol logic (those score < 8 and are fine to mock).
4. Land structured JSON-line logging (phase=spawn|snapshot|restore|verify,

5. Pin the migration with a regression test: take a snapshot of pane

## Acceptance

- Modifying WeztermClient or MockWezterm themselves.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:17.294418Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:31.177776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","session-persistence","snapshot","test"],"comments":[{"id":3655,"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi","author":"jemanuel","text":"[cc_2 progress note — fixture landed at 3598a9618; full migration deferred]

## Shipped
WeztermSubprocessFixture in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/common/wezterm_subprocess.rs
plus a 3-test smoke suite at tests/wezterm_subprocess_smoke.rs. Foundation
work that ft-2funa also needs.

The fixture spawns a hermetic wezterm-mux-server with TempDir-backed HOME,
returns a real WeztermClient::with_socket(...), and tears down on Drop.
Verified locally: FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 cargo test passes 3/3 (real mux
subprocess, list_panes returns the auto-spawned pane).

## Why the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration is NOT in this commit
The bead's headline ("convert to real WeztermSubprocessFixture") is bigger
than 1 hour. Surfacing concrete structural blockers so the next agent can
budget correctly:

1. **Pane-id semantics differ.** Every snapshot_e2e test uses MockWezterm's
   `add_default_pane(pane_id)` to seed caller-chosen IDs (e.g. 7, 11, 42).
   The real mux assigns pane IDs at spawn time. The smoke test confirms
   the auto-spawned default_prog pane is `pane_id=0`. Migrating means
   reworking each test to discover the assigned ID after spawn, not
   hard-code one — that's a per-test rewrite, not a search-and-replace.

2. **Output injection model is incompatible.** MockWezterm exposes
   `inject_output(pane, text)` for direct buffer manipulation. The real
   mux has no equivalent: output comes from the spawned shell's PTY.
   To produce deterministic content we'd need a custom shell program
   (e.g. spawn `/bin/sh -c 'printf "..."; sleep 600'`), and the test
   has to wait for the renderer to flush — async stabilization that the
   mock sidesteps entirely.

3. **Restore semantics assume process survival.** The tests at
   snapshot_e2e.rs:755, 1076, 1305, 1601, 1778, 2020 all do
   `restorer.restore(&session, &checkpoint, wezterm.clone())` using
   ONE Arc handle for both snapshot AND restore phases. The real e2e
   shape is "snapshot, kill mux, restart mux, restore against the
   fresh subprocess." Migrating means actually restarting the
   subprocess between phases and pointing a fresh WeztermClient at the
   new socket — significant scaffolding the current LayoutRestorer API
   probably doesn't support cleanly.

4. **Frame stabilization.** Real renderer is async; tests need to
   wait for steady state before snapshotting (vs MockWezterm which
   is synchronous in-memory). Need a `wait_until_idle(timeout)`
   helper on top of the fixture.

## Recommended next steps
- ft-dvgzi.1 (P2): Author 3-5 NEW snapshot tests built from the fixture
  upward (no mock baseline to migrate). Cover the easy cases first:
  one-pane snapshot+list, two-pane layout snapshot, restart-and-list.
  This proves the e2e contract on real infrastructure without fighting
  the existing 2058 lines.
- ft-dvgzi.2 (P3): Migrate snapshot_e2e.rs piecemeal, one test at a
  time, only after ft-dvgzi.1 surfaces the API gaps in LayoutRestorer.
- ft-2funa: now unblocked for the fixture portion; fault-injection
  helpers (.stop_pane, .pause_socket, .latency_inject) are still needed.

Leaving ft-dvgzi IN_PROGRESS so the .1 / .2 children can attach.","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:31Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-dvgzi.1","title":"[ft-dvgzi.1] author 3-5 new snapshot tests built from WeztermSubprocessFixture (one-pane, two-pane, restart+list)","description":"## Goal
Author 3-5 NEW snapshot integration tests that use the WeztermSubprocessFixture
landed in 3598a9618. Build the fixture-side e2e contract from scratch
rather than wrestling with the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration.

## Suggested test cases
1. **One-pane snapshot+list**: spawn, list_panes returns 1, snapshot
   serializes, deserialized snapshot retains the pane id.
2. **Two-pane layout**: spawn second pane via mux protocol, snapshot
   captures both, layout tree matches expected shape.
3. **Restart+list**: snapshot the fixture, drop+respawn the fixture
   under a fresh socket, attach a new WeztermClient, verify the
   restore path actually drives the new mux to recreate the pane
   (this is the critical e2e the current snapshot_e2e.rs tests do
   not exercise — they reuse the same Arc<MockWezterm>).
4. **Steady-state wait**: helper to poll wait_until_idle(timeout) so
   tests are not racy against the real renderer.

## Out of scope
- Migrating any of the 2058 lines in snapshot_e2e.rs.
- Modifying LayoutRestorer's API.

## Acceptance
- ≥3 NEW tests under tests/snapshot_real_mux.rs (or similar).
- All gated on FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 like the smoke suite.
- Structured JSON-line logging per the no-mocks skill.
- Each test < 5s wall time.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:51.857698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:51.857698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","snapshot","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi.1","depends_on_id":"ft-dvgzi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:51.857698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-dvgzi.2","title":"[ft-dvgzi.2] piecemeal migrate snapshot_e2e.rs MockWezterm tests to real WeztermSubprocessFixture","description":"## Goal
After ft-dvgzi.1 surfaces the API gaps in LayoutRestorer (probably
needs a way to point at a fresh mux subprocess after restart), migrate
the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs file ONE TEST AT A TIME to use the
WeztermSubprocessFixture.

## Why piecemeal
Each test in snapshot_e2e.rs uses MockWezterm's caller-chosen pane IDs
and synchronous output injection. Reworking each test requires:
- Discover real pane IDs post-spawn instead of hard-coding
- Use a deterministic shell program for content (not inject_output)
- Restart the mux subprocess between snapshot and restore phases
- Wait for renderer steady state before assertions

That's a per-test rewrite, not a mechanical replacement. P3 because
ft-dvgzi.1 delivers the no-mocks coverage value; this is migration
debt.

## Acceptance
- All 8+ snapshot_e2e tests run against real mux OR remain on
  MockWezterm with an explicit comment justifying why (e.g.,
  state-machine-only tests that score < 8 in the no-mocks Mock Risk
  matrix).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","snapshot","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dvgzi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi.2","depends_on_id":"ft-dvgzi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:52.152964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup. Verified via 4 new regression tests in fts_sync_tests (17 total passing): fts_sync_mode_indexes_on_insert (baseline), fts_deferred_mode_does_not_index_on_insert (post-DROP empty FTS), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_indexes_all_segments (500 segments → catchup → 500 indexed, second catchup is no-op), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_resumes_across_rounds (5 rounds × 100 segments interleaved with catchup, each round indexes exactly 100). Surfaced but did NOT fix: pre-existing seq=0 off-by-one in sync_fts_for_pane (WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq excludes fresh pane's seq=0 segment) — tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding.  {"id":"ft-wn3yc","title":"[reality-gap] default config export still exposes legacy wa-prefixed paths and prefixes under ft branding","description":"The shipped binary still exports several `wa`-prefixed defaults even though the docs and product surface are branded as `ft`.

Evidence from `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft config export --json` on 2026-04-23:
- `general.data_dir` defaults to `/Users/jemanuel/Library/Application Support/wa`
- `native.socket_path` defaults to `/tmp/wa/events.sock`
- `metrics.prefix` defaults to `wa`
- `notifications.email.subject_prefix` defaults to `[wa]`

This is a separate drift from the README example block: the binary itself still carries legacy default identity in operator-visible config output.

Expected fix:
- Audit whether these are intentional compatibility defaults or stale rename residue.
- Either update the defaults to `ft`-prefixed values or document the compatibility rationale explicitly in README/docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273929Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273302Z","close_reason":"Completed: default config export defaults rebranded to ft in bd6b39da and 5b16f248","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["branding","docs","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-wt9bh","title":"escape-parser: OSC 8 hyperlink Display still emits unescaped separators","description":"Production path: frankenterm/escape-parser/src/hyperlink.rs Display writes OSC 8 param keys/values and URI raw. Closed bead ft-mw1nw documented this, but the code still contains the TODOs and the regression test still asserts the broken parse failure. Impact: Display can emit id=a:b or URI values with semicolons that Hyperlink::parse splits as delimiters, dropping the entire hyperlink. Acceptance: either escape/decode OSC 8 params and URI separators or reject invalid values at construction/display with tests that prove Display -> parse round-trips or fails deliberately without emitting malformed OSC 8.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:40:33.113204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:08:19.556251Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:08:19.555857Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 19d2d5fa: OSC 8 Hyperlink Display percent-encodes field separators and parse decodes round-trip coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-wumpx","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:157: SPSC send_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_full","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpscProducer::send_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits a plain asupersync Notify at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:157. The function docs promise that cancellation during a blocked notified.await returns Err(value), but no select-race or cx-aware wait is used. If the queue is full and no consumer frees capacity after the caller cancels, the sender remains suspended until a not_full notification or close. This matches the known asupersync mid-flight-cancel gap for waits that are only checkpointed before suspension.

Expected fix direction: race notified.await against a cx-cancel watcher or introduce a shared cx-aware notify helper; add a LabRuntime regression where the queue is full, send_with_cx is blocked, cx is cancelled, and no receiver drains.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:06.919341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:14:32.782306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-wumpx","title":"[deadlock-audit] spsc_ring_buffer.rs:157: SPSC send_with_cx misses mid-flight cancel while waiting for not_full","description":"Audit: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer concurrency scan of crates/frankenterm-core/src.

Real hazard: SpscProducer::send_with_cx checks cx.checkpoint() before waiting, then awaits a plain asupersync Notify at spsc_ring_buffer.rs:157. The function docs promise that cancellation during a blocked notified.await returns Err(value), but no select-race or cx-aware wait is used. If the queue is full and no consumer frees capacity after the caller cancels, the sender remains suspended until a not_full notification or close. This matches the known asupersync mid-flight-cancel gap for waits that are only checkpointed before suspension.

Expected fix direction: race notified.await against a cx-cancel watcher or introduce a shared cx-aware notify helper; add a LabRuntime regression where the queue is full, send_with_cx is blocked, cx is cancelled, and no receiver drains.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:06.919341Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:35.294298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:35.294117Z","close_reason":"Fixed spsc send_with_cx mid-flight cancel via select-raced notify wait; rch spsc_ring_buffer tests passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wx5ln","title":"pattern pack subdirectories ignore supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:44.211855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.601212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.600868Z","close_reason":"Landed in 07e72a52: nested pack directories now discover supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests, with regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wxqbt","title":"mcp_missions::mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path: non-atomic write corrupts tx contract on mid-write crash","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs:289-308 mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path serializes a MissionTxContract and calls std::fs::write(path, json) directly. fs::write is NOT atomic — it open+truncate+write in a single sequence. If the process crashes (panic, SIGKILL, power loss, disk full mid-write) between truncate and final write, the target file is left truncated or with partial JSON. On next startup mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path reads the partial file, serde_json::from_str bails with 'Invalid tx contract JSON', and the ENTIRE tx contract is lost — not the last update, the whole in-flight contract. Same shape applies to mcp_save_mission_contract_to_path if it exists via a copy-paste. The codebase already has the atomic-rename pattern for durable writes (config_profiles.rs:129-135 manifest writes, recorder_storage.rs:1061, rulesets.rs:182, wal_engine.rs:892). Fix: write to path.with_extension('json.tmp') first, then std::fs::rename to the final path. rename is atomic on POSIX and NTFS — the tx file is either the old contents or the new contents, never a truncated halfway state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359416Z","close_reason":"Landed in 149f35d8 (fix(mcp_missions): atomic tmp+rename for mission/tx-contract saves). Both mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path and mcp_save_mission_to_path now write to <path>.json.tmp and std::fs::rename onto the target. Two regression tests (save_tx_contract_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt, save_mission_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt) assert target file presence + .tmp-sibling absence + round-trip parseability. Verification of the tests blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core compile blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wze7j","title":"[review][LOW] ft-szs5k exhaustive match arm contradicts pre-assert in events_labruntime test","description":"16ed98e5 added a pre-assert AND a silent Cancelled-or-Closed match arm in tests/events_labruntime.rs:198-213. Pre-assert at :200-203 uses matches!(result, Ok or Err Lagged) and panics with 'unexpected recv result' on anything else. Match at :212 accepts Cancelled or Closed silently. If either surfaced, the pre-assert fires FIRST — the match arm is unreachable dead code. Pre-commit state was cleaner: explicit match arm that panicked with 'unexpected close'. Likely caused by compiler warning non-exhaustive after fa1fbffe added Cancelled; the match was patched to add the exhaustive arm just to silence the warning without removing the intent. Pick either: (A) remove pre-assert, restore explicit panic arms, or (B) keep pre-assert, destructure via if-let. Severity: LOW, reviewer round 12, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, tests, cleanup.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:37.214441Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:59.965419Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:59.965100Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4a1860d0: remove unreachable pre-assert and keep explicit Cancelled/Closed panic arms","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
